body.content: Area-15 "The Final Stretch" is a renowned site known for their **consistent SSUs** and **active Site High Ranks**. SSUs are hosted every single day, with the average player count being around 20-60, an incredible achievement for a site their size. The site has recently hit **__500__** members, and is almost reaching **__600__**, a huge milestone for growing communities.
The site strives to achieve premier quality roleplay and growth as a site, with the goal of achieving 1,000 members by the end of Summer.

At Area 15, we value every contribution. Work alongside a fantastic team and be rewarded for your dedication. Together, we’ll safeguard humanity and make a real difference. With open positions, Well Designed Departments, and an Amazing Custom Map To Roleplay On. Welcome to the start of a new journey.
Astra's First Global SSU A Success

Turkish site Astra hosted their first Global SSU on the 20th of April, with the event being hailed a success by participants and management. This event marked the start of a new era for the group, which recently became a Verified Server within the Factions Hub.
We spoke with arzen473 (754710158931787846), Community Manager at Astra, about the event and about how their community has changed since joining the Factions Hub.

__**What happened during the SSU, what events took place, and was the SSU a success?**__
*No events were held during the first SSU because we wanted the staff to see the map and get used to it. Although the player count didn't quite meet our expectations […], it was still a very good session. The positive feedback from all participating Factions has motivated us.*

__**How has becoming a verified server changed your community?**__
*Initially, our founder’s goal was to open Astra and provide a fun space for Turkish SCP fans after getting bored with other Turkish servers. [O]nce we became verified, we expanded to Global. This transition had a big impact on our management staff; some were happy, some were surprised, and some were a bit frustrated. [W]e are continuing our [Turkish] operations so as not to cancel the roles of our Turkish members.*

body.alt: Players in Astra's SSU standing in a line.

body.content: It is always exciting to see new multicultural groups growing in the custom servers community, especially as these groups actively support cross-cultural engagement and diverse gameplay experiences.

It is certain that Astra has a bright future ahead of them in both the Turkish and wider community within SCP: Roleplay. Congratulations to them on this milestone and we eagerly await their next event.

body.label: Check out their Discord server here:

body.content: Verified Group of the Week: The Sarkic Union

The Sarkic Union, otherwise known as *Nälkänä Lej*, is a **custom, hostile GOI** based on Sarkicism. The faction was built on the question of "what if sarkics actually came together to do stuff". As any good Nälkäns, the Union love anything related to *fleshly stuff*, talking about how *insanely handsome* Grade Karcist Ion was, and *eating gods-*; all in hopes that the Union would eventually grow strong enough to bring back the Kalmaktama (Sarkic Empire) and defeat Yaldabaoth. To put it in less complicated terms, to save the universe from destruction! Well, if you ignore the fact that there's an 80% chance that the "saving" turns you into a silly fleshy creature.

Despite being a relatively small faction, the Union constantly tries to innovate! One of their cults (aka divisions), "Halkost", allows members to hop on as SK-BIO organisms, or otherwise known as silly creatures, an advancement that the Union is proud of.

Right now, we need to undergo a bit of a "rebirth" of sorts. With our only site we deploy in, "Site-64", growing less interesting for our players and simply just uh... not doing great- we're trying to find a new site to move into and call home! We've already got a few ideas, but it'll still take a bit to make the preparations and all. After that, off it is to making sure everything is in its best state possible for new members coming from the new site!

Dear readers, We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause, A potential cognitohazard has been identified within the content beyond this point. Continued exposure is not advised.
